# Neo Platform API

> Neo is the behavioral memory layer by Nura Labs. The Platform API lets you embed context management in your product with multi-tenant support.

## Base URL

https://neo.nura.sh/v1

## Authentication

Bearer token with account API key (prefix: sk-neo-acct-)

```
Authorization: Bearer sk-neo-acct-...
```

Create keys at /platform/keys or POST /v1/keys

## Required Headers (context operations)

- X-Neo-Workspace: platform workspace slug
- X-Neo-Tenant-Id: tenant external_id or slug

## Key Endpoints

### Account
- GET /v1/me — current account
- GET /v1/organization — organization details

### Workspaces
- GET /v1/workspaces — list workspaces
- POST /v1/workspaces — create workspace
- GET /v1/workspaces/{id} — get workspace

### Tenants
- GET /v1/tenants — list tenants
- POST /v1/tenants — create tenant (external_id, name)
- GET /v1/tenants/{id} — get tenant

### API Keys
- GET /v1/keys — list keys
- POST /v1/keys — create key (returns secret once)
- DELETE /v1/keys/{id} — revoke key

### Context API
- GET /v1/context/overview — workspace overview
- POST /v1/context/search — semantic search
- GET /v1/context/nodes — list nodes
- POST /v1/context/nodes — create node
- GET /v1/context/nodes/{id} — get node
- GET /v1/context/nodes/by-slug/{slug} — get by slug
- GET /v1/context/nodes/{id}/related — related nodes
- GET /v1/context/graph — knowledge graph

### Usage
- GET /v1/usage — organization usage
- GET /v1/usage/tenants/{tenant_id} — tenant usage

## OpenAPI

Full spec: https://neo.nura.sh/v1/openapi.json

## Documentation

Human-readable docs: https://nura.sh/neo/docs

## Quick Example

```bash
curl -s https://neo.nura.sh/v1/me \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $NEO_API_KEY"

curl -s https://neo.nura.sh/v1/context/search \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $NEO_API_KEY" \
  -H "X-Neo-Workspace: my-workspace" \
  -H "X-Neo-Tenant-Id: cust_123" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"query":"deployment process"}'
```

## Error Format

```json
{
  "error": {
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "message": "Description",
    "param": "field_name"
  },
  "request_id": "req_..."
}
```
